commands.review_summary:
The PR reclassifies Cloudflare challenge 403 HTML as upstream HTML and changes Codex OAuth model and WHAM usage requests to send browser-like User-Agent and browser headers.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +11. Total +44 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: Partly: source inspection reproduces the current status-403 HTML diagnostic path, but no live current-main or after-fix Codex OAuth Cloudflare challenge was exercised here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual provider-owner review is needed because the remaining blocker is auth-provider header direction and live proof, not a narrow mechanical repair.

target.title: fix: handle Cloudflare challenge errors for Codex OAuth calls
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where OpenAI/Codex OAuth users who are already logged in can hit Cloudflare browser-challenge HTML from `chatgpt.com/backend-api` and then see a misleading "Authentication failed" message.

The failure has two visible effects: the provider request may be blocked by Cloudflare, and when that block is returned as HTTP 403 HTML, OpenClaw currently routes users toward re-authentication instead of the existing CDN/gateway-blocked error path.

Related: #94432

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR keeps the Cloudflare challenge classifier fix and restores the Codex transport header mitigation that makes this branch distinct from the classifier-only PR in #94440.

The classifier now treats HTTP 403 HTML responses with Cloudflare browser-challenge markers as `upstream_html`, while generic 403 HTML still uses the existing auth-failure path.

Codex `chatgpt.com/backend-api` model calls now use a browser-like User-Agent and standard browser request headers. WHAM usage requests override the User-Agent after provider attribution resolution so the usage probe and model-call paths do not send inconsistent fingerprints to the same backend API family.

## User Impact

Users who hit a Cloudflare browser-challenge 403 HTML response now get a more accurate message that points to CDN/gateway blocking instead of being prompted to re-authenticate.

Codex OAuth model calls and WHAM usage probes also send browser-like headers to reduce the chance that `chatgpt.com/backend-api` treats OpenClaw as a non-browser client.

Generic 403 HTML responses continue to use the existing auth-failure classification.

## Evidence

Focused test run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.codex.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts
```

Result: passed.

Coverage includes:

- Cloudflare challenge HTML + 403 -> `upstream_html`
- Generic 403 HTML -> `auth_html`
- Codex model-call base headers include the browser-like User-Agent, `Accept`, and `Accept-Language`
- SSE and WebSocket builders preserve their transport-specific `Accept` behavior
- WHAM usage fetch and cooldown probe override User-Agent after provider attribution resolution

Live Codex OAuth behavior was not verified in this environment. This PR keeps the transport mitigation explicit so maintainers can decide whether the browser-like header direction is acceptable, instead of treating this as a duplicate of the classifier-only PR.

No screenshot is included because this is not a visual change.

AI-assisted: built with Codex
target.author: sunlit-deng
